channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: LLM Parameters for Math Across Languages: Shared or Separate?
item.description: arXiv:2606.18453v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit substantial cross-lingual variation in mathematical reasoning performance, but it remains unclear whether these differences reflect language-specific parameters or a shared mechanism that manifests differently by l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, arxiv, large language model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Montreal Forced Aligner and the state of speech-to-text alignment in 2026
item.description: arXiv:2606.18466v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Montreal Forced Aligner (MFA) was released in 2016 and has since become the most widely used tool for forced alignment in research and industry. In the decade since, MFA has undergone substantial development, including expanded coverage across mor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, research, release, arxiv, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Possible or Definite? A Benchmark for Evaluating Diagnostic Uncertainty Preservation in Clinical Text
item.description: arXiv:2606.18471v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for clinical text tasks such as summarization and revision. While most studies evaluate the fluency and coherence of LLM-generated text, whether LLMs correctly preserve diagnostic uncertainty remains ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> summarization, LLM, arxiv, vision, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CODEBLOCK: Learning to Supervise Code at the Right Granularity
item.description: arXiv:2606.18286v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Supervised fine-tuning of code LLMs typically applies uniform cross-entropy loss to all response tokens, implicitly assuming that every token provides equally useful learning signal. Recent token-level selection methods challenge this assumption in na...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG, framework, LLM, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DRIFT: Refining Instruction Data via On-Policy Data Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2606.18307v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Optimizing the training data distribution for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) dictates the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs). While existing data curation methods excel at accelerating training under constrained budgets, they are less suited to ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, fine-tuning, LLM, instruction, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SAGE: Retain-Aware Post-Hoc Sanitization of Final Unlearning Vector
item.description: arXiv:2606.18309v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) unlearning aims to remove undesirable knowledge or behaviors while preserving retained capabilities. Current unlearning methods all involve a trade-off between unlearning and retention. We have found that the retention activ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, large language model, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Searching for Synergy in Shared Workspace Human-AI Collaboration
item.description: arXiv:2606.18413v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated AI agents are increasingly capable, yet many scientific and professional tasks require human judgment and contextual expertise. We study shared-workspace human-AI teams, where AI agents and human collaborators must coordinate responsibilitie...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, arxiv, study, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimizing Lithium Production Decisions under Geological, Demand, and Pricing Uncertainties: A POMDP Framework for Multi-Objective Decision Making
item.description: arXiv:2606.18598v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decision making in lithium production is challenging, whether from an investor's perspective or a strategic production standpoint. Determining which mines to open and when to open them involves not only geological and price uncertainties, but also com...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, LLM, arxiv, product, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fisher Width: A Geometric Measure of Complexity on Statistical Manifolds
item.description: arXiv:2606.18306v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gaussian width is a central geometric complexity measure in high-dimensional probability, compressed sensing, convex optimization, and learning theory. It quantifies the average extent of a set along random directions, thereby capturing the effective ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Artemis: Anatomy-Resolved inTervention for Eliminating Multimodal NeuroImage confounderS
item.description: arXiv:2606.18287v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal neuroimaging, integrating functional connectivity from fMRI and structural connectivity from DTI, enables non-invasive analysis of brain networks using graph neural networks. However, demographic factors such as age and sex systematically c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, arxiv, experiment, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ForecastBench-Sim: A Simulated-World Forecasting Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2606.18686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forecasting benchmarks for general-purpose AI systems usually inherit the constraints of the real world: outcomes resolve slowly, tail events are rare, and counterfactual questions are difficult to score. We introduce ForecastBench-Sim, a simulated-wo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, release, arxiv, example, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: What Must Generalist Agents Remember?
item.description: arXiv:2606.18746v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper develops a formal account of what generalist agents must store in memory in order to act near-optimally across multiple environments and goals. It shows that when two domains share an observational bottleneck but require incompatible optima...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, model, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
